items.name: Uluwatu Temple
items.photo_tip: Capture the Kecak fire dance against the setting sun on the cliff edge.
items.description: Ancient sea temple perched on a steep cliff.

items.name: Tegallalang Rice Terrace
items.photo_tip: Morning light filtering through palm trees creates amazing ray effects.
items.description: Famous stepped rice paddies near Ubud.

items.name: Mason Adventures Rafting
items.photo_tip: Use a GoPro for action shots during the class 3 rapids.
items.description: White water rafting through the Ayung river valley.

items.name: Sisterfields Cafe
items.photo_tip: Outdoor seating area has the best natural light for food shots.
items.description: Australian-inspired cafe in the heart of Seminyak.

items.name: Potato Head Beach Club
items.photo_tip: The infinity pool at dusk makes for a perfect silhouette shot.
items.description: Architecturally stunning beach club with a massive lawn.

items.name: Campuhan Ridge Walk
items.photo_tip: Mist in the valley early morning adds a mystical feel.
items.description: Scenic hilltop walk through lush green valleys.

items.name: Locavore
items.photo_tip: Open kitchen allows for great chef-in-action shots.
items.description: Innovative fine dining using local Indonesian ingredients.

items.name: Finns Beach Club
items.photo_tip: VIP beds have best views of the ocean for lifestyle photos.
items.description: World-famous beach club in Canggu.

items.name: Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary
items.photo_tip: Try to catch monkeys playing near the ancient bridges and statues.
items.description: Natural habitat and sanctuary for Balinese long-tailed monkeys.

items.name: Motel Mexicola
items.photo_tip: The colorful interior and neon lights are iconic for Instagram.
items.description: Ultra-vibrant Mexican restaurant and bar in Seminyak.

items.name: Eiffel Tower
items.photo_tip: Shoot from Trocadéro Gardens for the best full-length view.
items.description: Iron lattice tower and global symbol of France.

items.name: The Louvre
items.photo_tip: The glass pyramid lights up beautifully just after sunset.
items.description: The world's largest art museum.

items.name: Le Comptoir de La Gastronomie
items.photo_tip: Low lighting gives a cozy Parisian vibe - use a wide aperture.
items.description: Classic French deli and bistro known for onion soup.

items.name: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
items.photo_tip: Morning light on the white dome is ethereal.
items.description: Historic bohemian hill with a stunning basilica.

items.name: Angelina Paris
items.photo_tip: Hot chocolate pour shots are a must here.
items.description: Iconic tearoom known for historic decor and hot chocolate.

items.name: Sainte-Chapelle
items.photo_tip: Wait for a sunny moment to see colors dancing on the floor.
items.description: Gothic chapel with spectacular 13th-century stained glass.

items.name: Bateaux Mouches Cruise
items.photo_tip: Paris at night from the river is magical; use a tripod if allowed.
items.description: Classic river cruise along the Seine.

items.name: Pink Mamma
items.photo_tip: The top floor sunroom is the most Instagrammed restaurant spot in Paris.
items.description: Famous 4-story Italian trattoria with stunning decor.

items.name: Shakespeare and Company
items.photo_tip: Capture the iconic facade before the afternoon crowds arrive.
items.description: World-famous English-language bookstore.

items.name: Musée d'Orsay
items.photo_tip: Silhouette shots through the giant clock face are iconic.
items.description: Impressionist art museum housed in a grand railway station.

items.name: Shibuya Crossing
items.photo_tip: Use a slow shutter speed from the Starbucks window to capture motion.
items.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing.

items.name: teamLab Borderless
items.photo_tip: Wear white clothing to act as a canvas for the projections.
items.description: Immersive digital art museum.

items.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
items.photo_tip: Close-ups of chefs preparing sushi make for great culinary content.
items.description: Bustling market for street food and fresh seafood.

items.name: Senso-ji Temple
items.photo_tip: The temple illuminated at night is much more serene than the daytime crowds.
items.description: Tokyo's oldest temple in Asakusa.

items.name: Meiji Jingu Shrine
items.photo_tip: The giant torii gates at the entrance provide a grand scale for photos.
items.description: Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji.

items.name: Harajuku Takeshita Street
items.photo_tip: The colorful street food and quirky fashion are perfect for lifestyle shots.
items.description: The epicenter of Tokyo's teenage street fashion.

items.name: Park Hyatt Tokyo (New York Bar)
items.photo_tip: The skyline view from the window is spectacular - classic 'Lost in Translation' vibe.
items.description: High-end bar with floor-to-ceiling windows.

items.name: Yayoi Kusama Museum
items.photo_tip: The pumpkin installation is the highlight; book weeks in advance.
items.description: Museum dedicated to the world-renowned avant-garde artist.

items.name: Akihabara Electric Town
items.photo_tip: Go deep into the side streets for the best neon glow shots.
items.description: Global hub for electronics, anime, and manga.

items.name: Ichiran Ramen Shinjuku
items.photo_tip: The individual dining booths are a unique architectural feature to photo.
items.description: Legendary ramen chain famous for its solo dining booths.
